Origin
Character Creation
Origin
This is the Character Creator. You can navigate to the different aspects of character creation by selecting the tabs at the top of the page. Once you have finished character creation, press Finish to populate this character's stats.On this page you can set your character's origins including their name, their primary element, and ancestry.
⟩⟩
 Adv. Pts
Pts 0 / 0
 Trn. Pts
Pts 0 / 0
 Perk Pts
Pts 0 / 0
▾ ▸
Origin
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Background
Sheet Name
Sheet Name
The name of the character sheet. This is the name of the character as written in the character's Journal entry.
Sheet Name
Sheet Name
The name of the character sheet. This is the name of the character as written in the character's Journal entry.
Full Name
Full Name
A character's full name. This is for self referential use and is not used anywhere except on this character sheet page.
Ancestry
Ancestry
Ancestry represents your characters species and background. This unlocks certain techniques and styles which are locked to specific ancestries.
Ethnicity
Ethnicity
The ethnicity the character is a part of.
Elemental Affinity
Elemental Affinity
Characters that are able to cast spells have an elemental affinity that ties them to one of the five primary elements. Some techniques require an elemental affinity before they may be taken.Your chosen affinity grants weaknesses and resistances to certain elemental damage types.
Quick Description
Quick Description
A short description of what this character is about
Title
Title
This is a title given to the character to briefly describe them. Shown when an introduction call is made.
Age
Age
This represents how old the character is in years.
Gender
Gender
The gender the character identifies as.
Home Region
Home Region
The region of Wuxing where the character hails from.
Backstory
Backstory
This is the background story of your character. Add any details on the character's past here.
?
Advancement
Advancement
As a character adventures, learns, and grows, they become more experienced in the world. Advancement tracks this progress through the character's level and experience. Here you can set your character's level. You may also spend any advancement points gained from increasing your character level on additional build points for jobs, skills, or techniques.
Character Level
Character Level
A trait that determines a character's general level of experience in the world. It increases as a character receives experience points (XP).
CR
CR
Your character rank applies to many of the values you record on your character sheet. This value limits the maximum tier of techniques a style can grant and also increases many statistics.Usually, this value is equal to its maximum value. It may be reduced in special situations where power is restricted. For example, a character may have the experience of a higher leveled character but lacks the power. Alternatively they may simply be holding back or are depowered for whatever reason.
Potency
Potency
This value is used as a bonus to trained skills, as a bonus to most damage, and as a cap to the amount EN you are able to use on a technique.
?
Influences
Influences
An influence is a phrase or desription of something of some importance to the character. An influence is written as a few words to describe a personality trait, bond, or ideal. Each Influence should be clearly defined and unique. Descriptors like “Skilled” or “Determined” are too general, since they could fit nearly any context. Instead, choose something more distinct, like “Street Duelist” or “Focused Studier.” These influences can then be further explained in a description for when it would be used if necessary.
Using Influences
Influences can be used to better perform skill checks. Whenenver a character makes a skill check, if they feel like the skill check is related to an influence they may draw on the influence to gain advantage. The number of advantages gained are equal to the influence's severity. Using an influence in this way always closts a Boon.Whenever you play your character and leverage an influence you may gain a Boon of Truth. Gaining this boon is usually reserved for particularly dramatic uses of your influence.Influences are used to modify social checks. Whenenver making a check that modifies favor, if your statements reference your target's influences it may grant you advantage or disadvantage to your check.Influences also modify persuade checks. Whenever performing a persuade check you will add or removed 1d6 to the check result. This value can increase by +5 on a moderate influence, or +10 on a severe.Only the most supportive influence and most opposing influence may affect any one persuade or favor roll.

Outfits

?
Language Select
Language Select
Select your language from the options below. This will change how your message is displayed in chat and also show what language you are using.
?
Minere - Common
Minere
Walthair The common language used in Minerva and the lands surrounding it. Minere is made up of three root languages that were once spoken commonly amongst the coastal civilizations of the area.
?
Junal - Common
Junal
Aridsha Juno's major language and the official language of the Guidance. Those who live within or below the city of Juno are expected to learn and speak this language to fully integrate into society.
?
Apollen - Common
Apollen
Khem The common tongue of Apollo, this language uses a variety of symbols in written works. It is one of the oldest languages still in use in modern times. In the Blessed lands it is known as Mons, the language of the mountainous people, the Monsen.
?
Lib - Common
Lib
Colswei This language is commonly used in Liber. Its roots seem entirely locked to the region, however the age of the language itself are unclear due to it not being used in written form until much later than its equivalents.
?
Cert - Common
Cert
Ceres The most commonly spoken language in all of Ceres, this language is the original tongue used by the Choi clan. To most in modern times, this connection to the Choi clan is lost but it can often be made clear in the company of those of the clan.
?
Dustell - Aridsha
Dustell
Aridsha This language has many derrivatives across the tribes of the desert. However each version, while distinct in its pronunciations, share a common written form derrived from the Shira language. Due to its use throughout the desert, it is Juno's second most common language.
?
Muralic - Aridsha
Muralic
Aridsha The language of the Murali people in the deserts of Aridsha. Its roots in Shira are clear and may be even closer to the ancient language than Junal.
?
Shira - Ancient
Shira
Aridsha An old language that evolved into both the Junal and Muralic languages. Usage can still be found in ancient Guidance relics.
?
Ciel - Capitol
Ciel
Ceres This language is used almost exclusively by Clan Par, the historians of Ceres. Those who learn the language often do so to share in the clan's protection and love of the stories and values of Ceres.
?
Citeq - South West Ceres
Citeq
Ceres A spoken language used mostly by the Ceresian tribes that roam the south western plains of Ceres.
?
Manstan - Southern Ceres
Manstan
Ceres The spoken language used in Southern Ceresian tribes. It has mostly fallen out of favor due to many of the tribes in this location having settled into Sanctum life, however many who wish to remember older times still speak this language.
?
Salkan - North West Ceres
Salkan
Ceres An entirely spoken language used by the Ceresian tribes located in the North west Salkandu region. It is most commonly known as the language of Clan Han.
?
Sansic - Eastern Ceres
Sansic
Ceres The spoken language used in Eastern Ceresian tribes. It has mostly fallen out of favor due to many of the tribes in this location having settled into Sanctum life, however many who wish to remember older times still speak this language.
?
Silq - Western Ceres
Silq
Ceres The spoken language used in Western Ceresian forest tribes. This language is not often heard due to the forest peoples' exclusion from most of Ceres society in modern times.
?
Kleikan - Klef
Kleikan
Khem This spoken language was once the language of the Suntouched people that lived west of the Khembalung Mountain range. It's largely fallen out of favor but pockets of people still try to keep the language alive.
?
Shorespeak - East Sea
Shorespeak
Walthair A spoken language used by those living along the east coast and in island nations across the sea. While it has no written language, its similarities to both Minere and ancient Vulca make it easy to communicate across cultures regardless.
?
Verdeni - Verdant Key
Verdeni
Walthair The spoken language of the island of Verdant Key. Its peoples' isolation from most of modern society has kept this language in tact to ancient times.
?
Emotion - Animal/Spirit
Emotion
Special The language of animals and spirits. This language is incredibly simplistic as it is communicated entirely through emotions.
?
Empathy - Ancient Spirit
Empathy
Special A language of the spirits. This language is communicated through thought and ether, allowing creatures of any type to understand.
?
Jovean - Ancient
Jovean
Special The common language of Novus, this language has been used across the blessed lands for as long as its people were aware. In the mainland, it is known as the language of the civilization of Jove and has largely fallen out of use.
?
Mytikan - Ancient
Mytikan
Special The root language of both Novan and Apollen, Mytikan is a language lost to time in the mainland. Ancient relics still use the language in the Blessed Lands and as such isn't surprising to find it still learned by scholars in Novus society.

Training
Character Creation
Training
Characters can spend time learning new skills in their own free time. In this page you can track your progress learning and potentially gain ranks in knowledge or learn new techniques.
⟩⟩
 Build
Pts 0 / 0
▾ ▸
Training
?
Training Points
Training Points
PP is used to gain Training Points for your character. You can convert PP into Training Points by using the convert to TP button below.
Training Points
Training Points
TP is gained when a character spends time learning new knowledge, styles, or techniques. This is usually gained by practicing a task during freetime at a rate of 1 per day. You may gain an additional TP if a character devotes an entire day to training. Once a character reaches 60 TP, they may spend their TP to gain an advancement point.
To Training Point: 60
Convert To TP
?
Training
Training
The pursuit of skill and knowledge is an ever flowing river. Training is a system to track your progress in learning knowledge and new techniques apart from Advancement.Here you can set any training points your character may have gained prior to character creation. You may also immediately spend these points on further build points.
Training Points
Training Points
Training points are gained through training and whenever you level. You can spend training points on bonus build points for both knowledge and techniques. Whenever you gain 30 PP, you gain one Training Point.
/
Bonus Advancement Points
Bonus Advancement Points
Bonus advancement points are gained by spending TP - experience gained when performing tasks.Whenever you gain 60 TP, you gain one Advancement Point.
Style Points
Style Points
You can spend training points to gain Style Points. These style points can be used to learn a new style. You must spend 1 training points to gain 1 style point.
cost: 1 training point

Advancement
Character Creation
Advancement
As a character adventures, learns, and grows, they become more experienced in the world. Advancement tracks this progress through the character's level and experience.
⟩⟩
 Adv. Pts
Pts 0 / 0
 Perk Pts
Pts 0 / 0
▾ ▸
Advancement
Character Level
Experience
Experience
Experience is a resource that is gained after completing challenges in a plot. When you gain enough experience you level up.
To Level: 60
Convert To Levels
Character Level
Character Level
A trait that determines a character's general level of experience in the world. It increases as a character receives experience points (XP).
Max CR
Max CR
Your character rank applies to many of the values you record on your character sheet. Your max character rank also increases many of the points given at character creation and through advancement. Max character rank increases as you level. At 1st level, it begins at 1. At 4th Level it increases to 2. At 9th Level it increases to 3. At 15th Level it increases to 4. At 22nd Level it increases to 5. At 30th Level it increases to 6. At 39th Level it increases to 7. At 49th Level it increases to 8. At 60th Level it increases to 9.
Training Points
Training Points
Training Points
TP is gained when a character spends time learning new knowledge, styles, or techniques. This is usually gained by practicing a task during freetime at a rate of 1 per day. You may gain an additional TP if a character devotes an entire day to training. Once a character reaches 60 TP, they may spend their TP to gain an advancement point.
To Adv. Point: 60
Convert To AP
Bonus Advancement Points
Bonus Advancement Points
Bonus advancement points are gained by spending TP - experience gained when performing tasks.Whenever you gain 60 TP, you gain one Advancement Point.
Advancement
Advancement Points
Advancement Points
Advancement points are a currency used to gain more knowledges or styles. Player characters start with two advancement points and gain an additional point whenever they gain a level. You can gain bonus advancement points by gaining training points and converting them to advancement points after reaching 60 of them.
/
Style Points
Style Points
You can spend advancement points to gain Style Points. These style points can be used to learn a new style. You must spend 1 advancement points to gain 1 technique point.
cost: 2 advancement points
Knowledge Points
Knowledge Points
You can spend training points to gain Knowledge Points. These knowledge points can be used to increase tier in a job. You must spend 1 training points to gain 1 knowledge point.
cost: 1 advancement point
▾ ▸
Perks
Perks
Perk Points
Perk Points
Perks are bonuses granted to a character to represent special aspects of how they function as a character. To purchase a perk, select it from the list by checking its checkbox.There is a limit as to how many perks a character may have based on the points they have gained.
/
Job Points
Job Points
Job Points lets you pick additional jobs. You can only ever have one job equipped. You can swap jobs as a swift action that costs 3 EN.
cost: 2 perk points
Job Points lets you pick additional jobs. You can only ever have one job equipped. You can swap jobs as a swift action that costs 3 EN.
Skill Points
Skill Points
Skill Points are used to gain new skills or skill augments.
cost: 1 perk point
Skill Points are used to gain new skills or skill augments.
Character Perks
Bonus Vitality
Cost: 0 perk point, Max:
Gain +1 Vitality per rank. Player characters should start with at least 1 Vitality.
Bonus Potency
Cost: 0 perk point, Max:
Gain +1 Potency per rank.This perk is not intended for player characters.
Branch Perks
Second Affinity
▣ ▢
Cost: 1 perk point
Human Only. Gain a second elemental affinity.
Light Magic
▣ ▢
Cost: 3 perk point
Fire Affinity Only. You can now learn Light affinty techniques that can blind and create illusions.
Blood Magic
▣ ▢
Cost: 3 perk point
Metal Affinity Only. You can now learn Blood affinty techniques that both heal and paralyze targets.
Spirit Conduit
▣ ▢
Cost: 2 perk point
Unaspected Human Only. You can hold multiple spirits inside of your body. You are able to swap your active spirit as a swift action for the cost of 2 EN.
Corrosion Magic
▣ ▢
Cost: 3 perk point
Wood Affinity Only. You can now learn Corrosion affinity techniques granting access to acids and poisons.
Shadow Magic
▣ ▢
Cost: 3 perk point
Earth Affinity Only. You can now learn Shadow affinity techniques that can blind and hide.
Time Magic
▣ ▢
Cost: 3 perk point
Water Affinity Only. You can now learn Time affinity techniques that allow specialized movement actions.
Stat Boost Perks
Increase HP
Cost: 1 perk point, Max:
Gain 10 HP
Increase Base Speed
Cost: 2 perk point, Max:
Gain +1 Base Speed
Increase Armor
Cost: 1 perk point, Max:
Gain +1 Armor
Bonus Equipment Slots
Cost: 1 perk point, Max:
Gain +1 Equipment Slot
Increase WILL
Cost: 1 perk point, Max:
Gain 10 WILL
Increase Dash Speed
Cost: 2 perk point, Max:
Gain +1 Dash Speed
Increase Starting EN
Cost: 2 perk point, Max:
Gain +1 Starting EN
▾ ▸
Perk Techniques
Filter Options
▣ ▢
Show Restricted Aspect Techniques
Show Restricted Aspect Techniques
Many techniques require the user to have a specific elemental aspect to use it. By default, the filter options below will not show any techniques you cannot use. Activating this option will not hide techniques you cannot learn because of your lack of the appropriate elemental aspect.
▣ ▢
Show Techniques Above Your Level
Show Techniques Above Your Level
Many techniques require a minimum level to be learned. By default, the filter options below will not show any techniques you are too low level to learn. Activating this option will not hide techniques you are too low leveled to learn.
Choose a Perk Technique Filter
Cost 2 Perks
Cost 3 Perks
Cost 4 Perks
Learned Perks
◉ Inspect
✘ Delete

Jobs

Advancement

Knowledge
Styles

Jobs
Origin
Finish
?
Jobs
Character Creation
Jobs
Jobs are a unique type of Style which broadly represents a character's role. A job will always grant bonuses to a character's combat or social stats, defenses, and special techniques to determine how the character acts in a conflict. When entering a conflict, only one job may be set at a time. On this page, you can see the number of job points you have available to spend on the left column. Each time you spend a job point you may gain a rank in one job. A job's maximum rank is equal to your Character Rank.Gaining a rank in a job often grants new techniques to use when a job's techniques are active.You gain a number of job points equal to your Character Rank. You may choose to gain additional job points by spending advancement points on level up.
⟩⟩
 Build
Pts 0 / 0
▾ ▸
Combat Jobs
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Vanguard
Frontline attackers with strong defenses and good self sustain.
▣ ▢ Fighter
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: None   A high survival weapon specialist that gains bonuses by being healed.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Brawler
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Physique   A melee mobile attacker that creates and exploits weaknesses in their foes to overwhelm them.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Labourer
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: None   A brawny worker with strong sustain abilities allowing them to endure powerful abilities and maintain focus.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Defender
Defensive jobs with abilities to protect allies from taking damage.
▣ ▢ Sentinel
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★★ Main Skills: Martial   A mobile melee attacker that intercepts attacks and retaliates against the foe.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Warden
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Channel   A mage that creates mantles around themselves and allies to reduce or negate incoming damage.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Bulwark
Category: Combat, World Difficulty: ★★★ Main Skills: Conjure   A defensive magic specialist that creates walls to protect allies and provide cover.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Athlete
Highly mobile jobs with abilities to dart in and out of a conflict.
▣ ▢ Exemplar
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Martial   A mobility specialist that concentrates ki into a burst to attack and maneuver foes.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Kineticist
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Kinesis   A ranged attacker that uses kinetic magic to force their foes to the ground and retain high mobility with kinetic attacks.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Rogue
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: None   A cloaking specialist that attacks in melee to exploit damage before ducking back into cover.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Ranger
These jobs have a focus on staying at range to avoid engaging in melee.
▣ ▢ Hunter
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: Any Range; Search   A ranged attacker that chooses a quarry to hunt down and focus fire.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Sniper
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: None   A long range attacker that wants to gain as much distance as possible to attack from safety.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Warmage
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: None   An area attacker that specializes in magic that attacks large groups of people.
See Techniques
▾ ▸
Utility Jobs
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Operator
Area control specialists that can modify the battlefield to maintain control.
▣ ▢ Trooper
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★★ Main Skills: Any Range   A ranged specialist that assigns areas to overwatch and prepare attacks for those that enter their area.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Geomancer
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Shape   A terrain molding job that gains benefits when foes enter their areas of control.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Magus
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ★★★ Main Skills: Resonance; Channel   A mage specializing in environmental manipulation, they create fields of arcane energy to hinder foes.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Spellwright
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ★★★ Main Skills: Shape   A conjuration mage that shapes material into new forms, allowing them to maintain magic that creates objects and changes the terrain.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Strategist
Support jobs to buff allies and provide unique opportunities.
▣ ▢ Tactician
Category: Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Analyze   A battlefield commander that repositions their allies into the optimal locations to attack.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Bard
Category: World, Social Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Inspire   An entertainer that gives advantages to allies and restores their willpower.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Healer
Jobs that bolster ones healing capabilities.
▣ ▢ Medic
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: Medicine   A powerful healer with the use of a medkit that can heal wounds as soon as they are made.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Alchemist
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Throw   A master of consumables that can aid allies with healing and hinder foes with bombs.
See Techniques
▾ ▸
World Jobs
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Advocate
These experts at conversation help pressure targets into agreeing to their requests.
▣ ▢ Merchant
Category: Social Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: Any Persuade   A social expert that convinces others of their requests through charming bargains and tact.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Courtier
Category: Social Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: Any Persuade; Any Cunning   A socialite that uses misdirection to imply and persuade others into following their requests.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Diplomat
Category: Social Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: Any Persuade; Any Cunning   A negotiator that uses pressure tactics to break down will and accelerate a deal.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Perceptor
Jobs that excel at detecting changes among people and the environment.
▣ ▢ Rogue
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ★☆☆ Main Skills: None   A cloaking specialist that attacks in melee to exploit damage before ducking back into cover.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Scholar
Category: World, Social Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Rationalize; Analyze   A collector of lore that gains bonuses when using their information to inform and detect lies.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Detective
Category: World Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Search   A seeker of clues and mysteries that gains expanded searching abilities and passive search bonuses.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Inquisitor
Category: Social Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Analyze   An investigator that analyzes people to learn their influences and find the truth.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Preacher
Category: Social Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: Empathy   An emotion expert that calms stress from will attacks and protects from those that would manipulate.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Craftsman
Jobs that help create objects and consumables.
▣ ▢ Culinarian
Category: World Difficulty: ★★☆ Main Skills: None   A chef that grants benefits to those that consume their meals.
See Techniques
⊙ Show
⊕ Hide
Esper
Spirit focused jobs that either allow communication with spirits or expand spirit options.
▣ ▢ Etherealist
Category: World, Social Difficulty: ☆☆☆ Main Skills: Resonance; Empathy   A master of spirit communication that can transport themselves to the ethereal plane.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Phantasm
Category: World, Social Difficulty: ☆☆☆ Main Skills: Resonance; Charm   Spirit Only. Spirits that develop their abilities to manipulate emotion and thought via distracting hallucinations.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Shade
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ☆☆☆ Main Skills: Resonance   Spirit Only. A spirit that can manifest itself into objects and shadows to scout and attack.
See Techniques
▣ ▢ Yaksa
Category: World, Combat Difficulty: ☆☆☆ Main Skills: Resonance; Shape   Spirit Only. Powerful nature spirits that tend to remain in a domain and protect the region they call home.
See Techniques

?
Attributes
Character Creation
Attributes
Attributes are the inherent characteristics of your character. Characters have a numerical rating for each attribute, which determines the modifier they grant to skills and affect their derived stats. Attributes range from a +3 bonus to a -1 penalty. Whenever you raise an attribute to a rank you spend an equal number of attribute points.By reducing an attribute below 0, you gain an equal number of attribute points. At most, a character can gain only one attribute point in this way.You gain a number of attribute points equal to 6 + your Character Rank.
⟩⟩
 Attr. Pts
Pts 0 / 0
 Skill Pts
Pts 0 / 0
▾ ▸
Attributes
Body
i
Body
Body is a representation of your overall health and physical strength. It is used as a base stat for feats of physical strength or when the body must be tested.
Precision
i
Precision
Precision is how flexible and accurate you are with your body. It is used for feats that require careful aim or stability.
Quickness
i
Quickness
Quickness is your reflexes and your ability to react quickly to situations. It is used when quick reactions are required.
Conviction
i
Conviction
Conviction is how strongly you hold onto beliefs. It drives your resolve and helps with skills related to concentration or strong wills.
Intuition
i
Intuition
Intuition is how quickly you can assess a situation and come to conclusions. It is the basis of your ego and helps in situations that require quick thinking and perception.
Reason
i
Reason
Reason is how you assess information. It helps you when logic or rational thinking is required for a skill and helps in your ability to recall information.
▾ ▸
Skills
Active Skills
Athletics
▣ ▢ Agility (PRC/QCK)
Agility
Athletics Agility is an active skill used for feats of dexterity and reflex. This skill will most often find use whenever a technique requires swift or reflexive action such as quickly dodging an attack or keeping your balance.
Agility
▣ ▢ Agility (PRC/QCK)
Agility
Athletics Agility is an active skill used for feats of dexterity and reflex. This skill will most often find use whenever a technique requires swift or reflexive action such as quickly dodging an attack or keeping your balance.
Agility
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Aim (PRC)
Aim
Athletics Aim is an active skill that allows one to pinpoint targets at a distance. It's commonly used with ranged weapon techniques and ranged spells.
Aim
▣ ▢ Aim (PRC)
Aim
Athletics Aim is an active skill that allows one to pinpoint targets at a distance. It's commonly used with ranged weapon techniques and ranged spells.
Aim
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Martial (BOD/PRC)
Martial
Athletics Martial is an active skill for striking characters with melee weapons. It is exclusively an attacking skill for techniques that benefit from precise strikes to maximize effectiveness.
Martial
▣ ▢ Martial (BOD/PRC)
Martial
Athletics Martial is an active skill for striking characters with melee weapons. It is exclusively an attacking skill for techniques that benefit from precise strikes to maximize effectiveness.
Martial
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Physique (BOD)
Physique
Athletics Physique is an active skill used for feats of strength using the body. This skill comes into play when a technique requires raw power such as punching, shoving, jumping, climbing, or lifting.
Physique
▣ ▢ Physique (BOD)
Physique
Athletics Physique is an active skill used for feats of strength using the body. This skill comes into play when a technique requires raw power such as punching, shoving, jumping, climbing, or lifting.
Physique
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
Magic
▣ ▢ Energize (BOD/CNV)
Energize
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Energize is a casting skill to create and maintain control over energies. It is most often used when concentrating on maintaining an energy you've created like fire, lighting, or cold.
Energize
▣ ▢ Energize (BOD/CNV)
Energize
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Energize is a casting skill to create and maintain control over energies. It is most often used when concentrating on maintaining an energy you've created like fire, lighting, or cold.
Energize
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Flux (PRC)
Flux
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Flux is a casting skill used to create and manipulate forces. This is most often used to move objects but can also generate wind or apply forces to an area like a gravitational effect.
Flux
▣ ▢ Flux (PRC)
Flux
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Flux is a casting skill used to create and manipulate forces. This is most often used to move objects but can also generate wind or apply forces to an area like a gravitational effect.
Flux
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Materialize (BOD/CNV)
Materialize
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Materialize is a casting skill that creates matter by changing ambient ether into a desired form then stabilizes it. The material you create is determined by your elemental affinity(s). Wood creates pine, fire creates glass, earth creates granite, metal creates iron, and water creates ice.
Materialize
▣ ▢ Materialize (BOD/CNV)
Materialize
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Materialize is a casting skill that creates matter by changing ambient ether into a desired form then stabilizes it. The material you create is determined by your elemental affinity(s). Wood creates pine, fire creates glass, earth creates granite, metal creates iron, and water creates ice.
Materialize
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Physio (QCK/INT)
Physio
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Physio is a casting skill used to modify creatures and life. It is most often used for healing abilities but can also apply muscle stimulants to grant feats of physical strength.
Physio
▣ ▢ Physio (QCK/INT)
Physio
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill. Physio is a casting skill used to modify creatures and life. It is most often used for healing abilities but can also apply muscle stimulants to grant feats of physical strength.
Physio
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Transmute (PRC/RSN)
Transmute
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill and available material to affect of which you have an elemental affinity it is composed of. This skill allows you to reshape the material into new forms like modifying terrain or changing the shape of dust into objects. This skill cannot modify material that is being resonated by another mage.
Transmute
▣ ▢ Transmute (PRC/RSN)
Transmute
Magic You must have an elemental aspect to use this skill and available material to affect of which you have an elemental affinity it is composed of. This skill allows you to reshape the material into new forms like modifying terrain or changing the shape of dust into objects. This skill cannot modify material that is being resonated by another mage.
Transmute
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
Social Skills
Persuade
▣ ▢ Charm (INT)
Charm
Persuade Charm is a social skill used when attempting to meet another emotionally. This can be done through empathy, support, romance, and allure. Charm is used both for persuasion and deception, as long as the attempt is meant to appeal emotionally.
Charm
▣ ▢ Charm (INT)
Charm
Persuade Charm is a social skill used when attempting to meet another emotionally. This can be done through empathy, support, romance, and allure. Charm is used both for persuasion and deception, as long as the attempt is meant to appeal emotionally.
Charm
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Inspire (CNV)
Inspire
Persuade Inspire is a social skill used to evoke feelings of hope and to counter dispair. Inspire is used when motivating action. Techniques that use inspire often provide emotional boosts and bolster willpower. Inspire is also often used in persuasion attempts to convince another to help.
Inspire
▣ ▢ Inspire (CNV)
Inspire
Persuade Inspire is a social skill used to evoke feelings of hope and to counter dispair. Inspire is used when motivating action. Techniques that use inspire often provide emotional boosts and bolster willpower. Inspire is also often used in persuasion attempts to convince another to help.
Inspire
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Rationalize (RSN)
Rationalize
Persuade Rationalize is a social skill used when persuading another through reason and logic. Rationalize is usually a tool for negotiation when appealing to a logical mindset such as when pragmatism is necessary. It can also be used to deceive using misinformation and selling it as truth.
Rationalize
▣ ▢ Rationalize (RSN)
Rationalize
Persuade Rationalize is a social skill used when persuading another through reason and logic. Rationalize is usually a tool for negotiation when appealing to a logical mindset such as when pragmatism is necessary. It can also be used to deceive using misinformation and selling it as truth.
Rationalize
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
Cunning
▣ ▢ Demoralize (CNV)
Demoralize
Cunning Demoralize is a social skill associated with negativity and intimidating behaviour. Techniques that key off this skill usually attack willpower and invoke many negative emotions to control others' behaviour.
Demoralize
▣ ▢ Demoralize (CNV)
Demoralize
Cunning Demoralize is a social skill associated with negativity and intimidating behaviour. Techniques that key off this skill usually attack willpower and invoke many negative emotions to control others' behaviour.
Demoralize
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Misdirect (INT/RSN)
Misdirect
Cunning Misdirect is a social skill used to obscure information through lies or omitting facts. Misdirect is most often applicable when using outright lies or tactics that try to redirect a conversation or one's attention.
Misdirect
▣ ▢ Misdirect (INT/RSN)
Misdirect
Cunning Misdirect is a social skill used to obscure information through lies or omitting facts. Misdirect is most often applicable when using outright lies or tactics that try to redirect a conversation or one's attention.
Misdirect
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
World Skills
Craft
▣ ▢ Aesthetics (INT)
Aesthetics
Craft Aesthetics is the ability to design. This could be used to make an art piece more appealing or make a chair more comfortable to sit on.
Aesthetics
▣ ▢ Aesthetics (INT)
Aesthetics
Craft Aesthetics is the ability to design. This could be used to make an art piece more appealing or make a chair more comfortable to sit on.
Aesthetics
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Alchemy (RSN)
Alchemy
Craft Alchemy is a technical skill used when making alchemical creations. These creations typically take the form of medicines, poisons, salves, oils, and other consumable crafts.
Alchemy
▣ ▢ Alchemy (RSN)
Alchemy
Craft Alchemy is a technical skill used when making alchemical creations. These creations typically take the form of medicines, poisons, salves, oils, and other consumable crafts.
Alchemy
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Build (RSN)
Build
Craft Build is an active skill used whenever you are creating an object through precise technique. This skill is used mostly when you wish to create through the use of tools but some complicated items will require build to represent the precision required in the object being created.
Build
▣ ▢ Build (RSN)
Build
Craft Build is an active skill used whenever you are creating an object through precise technique. This skill is used mostly when you wish to create through the use of tools but some complicated items will require build to represent the precision required in the object being created.
Build
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Cook (QCK/CNV)
Cook
Craft Cook is a technical skill used when dealing with ingredients to be used for consumption and nutrition. This skill is not only used for traditional cooking but anytime food is prepared such as creating beverages. Food is an important resource to survive and keep spirits high.
Cook
▣ ▢ Cook (QCK/CNV)
Cook
Craft Cook is a technical skill used when dealing with ingredients to be used for consumption and nutrition. This skill is not only used for traditional cooking but anytime food is prepared such as creating beverages. Food is an important resource to survive and keep spirits high.
Cook
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
Device
▣ ▢ Glyphwork (QCK/RSN)
Glyphwork
Device Glyphwork is a technical skill that allows one to create and manipulate pnevmarite technology. If a device requires magical tooling, this skill will often be used to work with it.
Glyphwork
▣ ▢ Glyphwork (QCK/RSN)
Glyphwork
Device Glyphwork is a technical skill that allows one to create and manipulate pnevmarite technology. If a device requires magical tooling, this skill will often be used to work with it.
Glyphwork
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Medicine (QCK/INT)
Medicine
Device Medicine is used when applying medical procedures like stitching a wound or using curatives.
Medicine
▣ ▢ Medicine (QCK/INT)
Medicine
Device Medicine is used when applying medical procedures like stitching a wound or using curatives.
Medicine
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Musicianship (PRC)
Musicianship
Device Musicianship is the ability to perform with a musical instrument.
Musicianship
▣ ▢ Musicianship (PRC)
Musicianship
Device Musicianship is the ability to perform with a musical instrument.
Musicianship
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Pilot (BOD/CNV)
Pilot
Device Pilot is an active skill used to maneuver vehicles and machinery. Most complicated machinery will require a pilot check to utilize the device any many techniques with pilot allow you to perform complicated maneuvers with pilot.
Pilot
▣ ▢ Pilot (BOD/CNV)
Pilot
Device Pilot is an active skill used to maneuver vehicles and machinery. Most complicated machinery will require a pilot check to utilize the device any many techniques with pilot allow you to perform complicated maneuvers with pilot.
Pilot
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Tinker (PRC/QCK)
Tinker
Device Tinker is a technical skill that allows one to create and manipulate machinery and technology. This skill can be used to disarm traps and locks. Otherwise, this will also be used when constructing complicated technology that require gears, pulleys, or other devices that require moving parts.
Tinker
▣ ▢ Tinker (PRC/QCK)
Tinker
Device Tinker is a technical skill that allows one to create and manipulate machinery and technology. This skill can be used to disarm traps and locks. Otherwise, this will also be used when constructing complicated technology that require gears, pulleys, or other devices that require moving parts.
Tinker
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
Perception
▣ ▢ Analyze (RSN)
Analyze
Perception Analyze is a technical skill used when information must be parsed quickly and efficiently. This skill is often used to find hidden clues that would not be in plain sight. It is also used whenever one wishes to quickly learn about a subject written in text or requires one to reason through a problem.
Analyze
▣ ▢ Analyze (RSN)
Analyze
Perception Analyze is a technical skill used when information must be parsed quickly and efficiently. This skill is often used to find hidden clues that would not be in plain sight. It is also used whenever one wishes to quickly learn about a subject written in text or requires one to reason through a problem.
Analyze
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Empathy (INT)
Empathy
Perception Empathy is a social skill used to actively detect another's emotional state. Empathy will often be used to sense another's convictions and influences. It can also see use when seeing through emotional deception.
Empathy
▣ ▢ Empathy (INT)
Empathy
Perception Empathy is a social skill used to actively detect another's emotional state. Empathy will often be used to sense another's convictions and influences. It can also see use when seeing through emotional deception.
Empathy
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Notice (INT)
Notice
Perception Notice is used to find the hidden and anything that can be found with one's sight, hearing, or smell. Usually this is used to find characters hiding but can also be used to find hidden objects.
Notice
▣ ▢ Notice (INT)
Notice
Perception Notice is used to find the hidden and anything that can be found with one's sight, hearing, or smell. Usually this is used to find characters hiding but can also be used to find hidden objects.
Notice
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise
▣ ▢ Resonance (BOD/CNV)
Resonance
Perception Resonance is a skill used to detect the presence of ether. This can be used to detect density of ether in an area but also can be used to determine if a material was made through ether. Complicated techniques can rely on resonance to remove the ether completely.
Resonance
▣ ▢ Resonance (BOD/CNV)
Resonance
Perception Resonance is a skill used to detect the presence of ether. This can be used to detect density of ether in an area but also can be used to determine if a material was made through ether. Complicated techniques can rely on resonance to remove the ether completely.
Resonance
▣ ▢ Skill Expertise

[CR ] Lv.
HP
Hit Points
Hit Points (HP) are the number of hits a character can take in combat. Your character’s hit points is a representation of your character maintaining their barrier to take hits, resisting harm with their toughness, and general ability to avoid harm. A character may be taking attacks from multiple sources that can be easily shrugged off, but once they run out of HP to do so is when the big hits make their way through.
Hit Points
WILL
Willpower
Willpower is a character's ability to stay invested in a situation.
Willpower
Surge
Surge
Surge is a resource that allows a character to tap into their resolve to continue a fight. It is most often spent to restore their HP and Willpower.Whenever a will break is caused, a Surge is consumed and the character's Willpower restores to full. If you cannot consume a Surge, your Willpower remains at 0. After completing a short rest, 1 surge is restored. After completing a full rest, all surge is restored.
Surge
Vitality
Vitality
Whenever you are reduced to zero HP, your HP restores to full and you lose one vitality. Unlike most resources, vitality can be reduced below 0.Whenever vitality is lost check if it is below 1. If it is, you gain the Downed status. If the character already had the Downed status, they also gain the Dying and Unconscious statuses which may ultimately kill them.Most characters will have a max vitality of 1. Player characters will usually start with 3 while special characters can have any amount.After completing a full rest, 1 vitality is healed.
Vitality
 
Defenses
Brace (BOD)
Brace
Defenses are used to defend against techniques used against you. Often these stats are compared against to determine what effects the technique can inflict upon you.Brace is your ability to resist a physical force by holding strong and blocking. It is most often associated with physical attacks or slow magical attacks.
Brace
Warding (PRC)
Warding
Defenses are used to defend against techniques used against you. Often these stats are compared against to determine what effects the technique can inflict upon you.Warding defense represents an attempt to disrupt the ether of a magical effect. It is most often associated with resisting energy attacks.
Warding
Evasion (QCK)
Evasion
Defenses are used to defend against techniques used against you. Often these stats are compared against to determine what effects the technique can inflict upon you.Evasion is your ability to completely evade an attack. Some especially slow attacks can be evaded and will check against evasion. Most physical damage will also compare against Evasion to determine if they can deal half damage to their target.
Evasion
Resolve (CNV)
Resolve
Defenses are used to defend against techniques used against you. Often these stats are compared against to determine what effects the technique can inflict upon you.Resolve is a representation of your courage and determination. It is used in cases when you need to hold firm to your convictions.
Resolve
Insight (INT)
Insight
Defenses are used to defend against techniques used against you. Often these stats are compared against to determine what effects the technique can inflict upon you.Insight is your sense of self and character. Most will damage will also compare against Insight to determine if they can deal half damage to their target.
Insight
Logic (RSN)
Logic
Defenses are used to defend against techniques used against you. Often these stats are compared against to determine what effects the technique can inflict upon you.Logic is your ability to assess a situation. It is used when seeing through lies or deeper meanings behind arguments.
Logic
 
Conflict Stats
Starting EN
Starting EN
EN is a resource that is spent when using more powerful techniques. Usually you will generate some at the start of every round in a combat. Some techniques can also generate EN.Start EN is how much EN you have when starting a conflict.
Starting EN
Round EN
Round EN
EN is a resource that is spent when using more powerful techniques. Usually you will generate some at the start of every round in a combat. Some techniques can also generate EN.Round EN is how much EN you gain at the start of a round during a conflict.
Round EN
Base Speed
Base Speed
During conflict, the first time you act during your turn you may move a number of spaces equal to your base speed. If you use any action other than Swift actions or Reactions you lose this movement. You also do not gain your base speed movement when starting additional turns in a round or when you are Downed.
Base Speed
Dash Speed
Dash Speed
Some techniques allow you to move a number of spaces equal to your Dash Speed. Generally, if a technique allows you to move as part of its effects, you will be referencing your Dash Speed to determine how far you move.
Dash Speed
Regen Value
Regen Value
This value is a standard amount of HP you recover from some healing abilities.
Regen Value
Armor
Armor
Armor reduces up to half of incoming HP damage from a single source by an amount equal to its rating. Armor is typically gained from gear, but techniques can grant armor temporarily.
Armor

Styles

Knowledge
Advancement

Knowledge
Styles
Attributes
Jobs
Origin
Finish
?
Knowledge
Character Creation
Knowledge
Knowledge represents information a character knows on a subject. Knowledge can be divided into two categories, languages and lore. Languages are divided by the locations of the world where they are used. Learning a language allows one to speak, read, and write the language. Lore is knowledge of a single broad topic. Whenever you use the Recall Knowledge technique, you will roll with the modifier of an appropriate lore knowledge for the subject you wish to recall knowledge for. When you make a lore check your modifier is equal to your lore's rank + your Character Rank.Lore is divided into categories based on the context of their usage. Each category has a General knowledge that can be trained. Normally, you cannot make a lore check without having an associated lore, however having the general knowledge of the subject will allow it. When making a general lore check, your modifier is equal to your Character Rank.On this page you can see the number of knowledge points you have available to spend on the left column. Each time you spend a knowledge point you may raise the rank of any lore or learn a language. To raise the rank of a lore, you may change the value of a lore with its dropdown. To learn a language you must check the language off on the checkbox.You gain a number of skill points equal to 6 + your Character Rank. You may choose to gain additional knowledge points by spending training points through training on level up.
⟩⟩
 Build
Pts 0 / 0
▾ ▸
Language
Common Languages
▣ ▢ Minere
Minere
Walthair The common language used in Minerva and the lands surrounding it. Minere is made up of three root languages that were once spoken commonly amongst the coastal civilizations of the area.
- Walthair
▣ ▢ Junal
Junal
Aridsha Juno's major language and the official language of the Guidance. Those who live within or below the city of Juno are expected to learn and speak this language to fully integrate into society.
- Aridsha
▣ ▢ Apollen
Apollen
Khem The common tongue of Apollo, this language uses a variety of symbols in written works. It is one of the oldest languages still in use in modern times. In the Blessed lands it is known as Mons, the language of the mountainous people, the Monsen.
- Khem
▣ ▢ Lib
Lib
Colswei This language is commonly used in Liber. Its roots seem entirely locked to the region, however the age of the language itself are unclear due to it not being used in written form until much later than its equivalents.
- Colswei
▣ ▢ Cert
Cert
Ceres The most commonly spoken language in all of Ceres, this language is the original tongue used by the Choi clan. To most in modern times, this connection to the Choi clan is lost but it can often be made clear in the company of those of the clan.
- Ceres
⊕ Hide
⊙ Show
Walthair Languages
▣ ▢ Shorespeak
Shorespeak
Walthair A spoken language used by those living along the east coast and in island nations across the sea. While it has no written language, its similarities to both Minere and ancient Vulca make it easy to communicate across cultures regardless.
- East Sea
▣ ▢ Verdeni
Verdeni
Walthair The spoken language of the island of Verdant Key. Its peoples' isolation from most of modern society has kept this language in tact to ancient times.
- Verdant Key
⊕ Hide
⊙ Show
Aridsha Languages
▣ ▢ Dustell
Dustell
Aridsha This language has many derrivatives across the tribes of the desert. However each version, while distinct in its pronunciations, share a common written form derrived from the Shira language. Due to its use throughout the desert, it is Juno's second most common language.
- Aridsha
▣ ▢ Muralic
Muralic
Aridsha The language of the Murali people in the deserts of Aridsha. Its roots in Shira are clear and may be even closer to the ancient language than Junal.
- Aridsha
▣ ▢ Shira
Shira
Aridsha An old language that evolved into both the Junal and Muralic languages. Usage can still be found in ancient Guidance relics.
- Ancient
⊕ Hide
⊙ Show
Khem Languages
▣ ▢ Kleikan
Kleikan
Khem This spoken language was once the language of the Suntouched people that lived west of the Khembalung Mountain range. It's largely fallen out of favor but pockets of people still try to keep the language alive.
- Klef
⊕ Hide
⊙ Show
Ceres Languages
▣ ▢ Ciel
Ciel
Ceres This language is used almost exclusively by Clan Par, the historians of Ceres. Those who learn the language often do so to share in the clan's protection and love of the stories and values of Ceres.
- Capitol
▣ ▢ Citeq
Citeq
Ceres A spoken language used mostly by the Ceresian tribes that roam the south western plains of Ceres.
- South West Ceres
▣ ▢ Manstan
Manstan
Ceres The spoken language used in Southern Ceresian tribes. It has mostly fallen out of favor due to many of the tribes in this location having settled into Sanctum life, however many who wish to remember older times still speak this language.
- Southern Ceres
▣ ▢ Salkan
Salkan
Ceres An entirely spoken language used by the Ceresian tribes located in the North west Salkandu region. It is most commonly known as the language of Clan Han.
- North West Ceres
▣ ▢ Sansic
Sansic
Ceres The spoken language used in Eastern Ceresian tribes. It has mostly fallen out of favor due to many of the tribes in this location having settled into Sanctum life, however many who wish to remember older times still speak this language.
- Eastern Ceres
▣ ▢ Silq
Silq
Ceres The spoken language used in Western Ceresian forest tribes. This language is not often heard due to the forest peoples' exclusion from most of Ceres society in modern times.
- Western Ceres
⊕ Hide
⊙ Show
Special Languages
▣ ▢ Emotion
Emotion
Special The language of animals and spirits. This language is incredibly simplistic as it is communicated entirely through emotions.
- Animal/Spirit
▣ ▢ Empathy
Empathy
Special A language of the spirits. This language is communicated through thought and ether, allowing creatures of any type to understand.
- Ancient Spirit
▣ ▢ Jovean
Jovean
Special The common language of Novus, this language has been used across the blessed lands for as long as its people were aware. In the mainland, it is known as the language of the civilization of Jove and has largely fallen out of use.
- Ancient
▣ ▢ Mytikan
Mytikan
Special The root language of both Novan and Apollen, Mytikan is a language lost to time in the mainland. Ancient relics still use the language in the Blessed Lands and as such isn't surprising to find it still learned by scholars in Novus society.
- Ancient
▾ ▸
Lore
Academic Lore
▣ ▢ General Academic
Academic
Academic This represents general education for academic study for the purposes of functioning in modern society.
Specialized Academic Lore
Profession Lore
▣ ▢ General Profession
Profession
Profession Profession is the general knowledge of any kind of job, what they do, and how it is performed.
Specialized Profession Lore
Craftmanship Lore
▣ ▢ General Craftmanship
Craftmanship
Craftmanship The knowledge of creating items through manipulation of substances and materials. A knowledge check here will help one identify techniques used to create an object but not necessarily how to recreate it.
Specialized Craftmanship Lore
Geography Lore
▣ ▢ General Geography
Geography
Geography Geography represents general knowledge of terrains and locations within an area.
Specialized Geography Lore
History Lore
▣ ▢ General History
History
History History knowledges represent known history of civilizations and any legends that may exist.
Specialized History Lore
Culture Lore
▣ ▢ General Culture
Culture
Culture Culture knowledge represents information on societal customs, art, and entertainment options.
Specialized Culture Lore
Religion Lore
▣ ▢ General Religion
Religion
Religion Religion knowledge represent known tenets, famous people and creatures of the religion, and information about legends, beliefs, and organizations.
Specialized Religion Lore

Styles
